message:
refactor(lib): migrate all shell libraries + entry scripts to the new passwd/user resolvers

Follow-on to the install.sh / generated-installer introduction of
acfs_*_system_binary_path / _resolve_current_user / _getent_passwd_entry
/ _passwd_home_from_entry. Each standalone entry point now owns a
namespaced copy of the same surface so every layer can resolve the
current user and their $HOME without depending on either `$PATH` or
on `getent` being present.

Per-file prefixes used in this commit:
- packages/onboard/onboard.sh                 → onboard_*
- scripts/acfs-global, scripts/acfs-update    → (unprefixed helpers,
  scoped to those two entry scripts)
- scripts/lib/install_helpers.sh              → _acfs_*
- scripts/lib/state.sh                        → state_*
- scripts/lib/cheatsheet.sh                   → cheatsheet_*
- scripts/lib/doctor.sh, doctor_fix.sh        → (reuse state_*/_acfs_*)
- scripts/lib/export-config.sh                → export_config_*
- scripts/lib/info.sh, status.sh              → (reuse state_*)
- scripts/lib/agents.sh, autofix.sh, cli_tools.sh, cloud_db.sh,
  dashboard.sh, github_api.sh, languages.sh, nightly_update.sh,
  notifications.sh, notify.sh, os_detect.sh, stack.sh, support.sh,
  ubuntu_upgrade.sh, update.sh, user.sh, webhook.sh, zsh.sh,
  changelog.sh, continue.sh, smoke_test.sh                       →
  swap in-file `getent passwd ... | cut -d: -f6` / `whoami` /
  `id -un` call sites for the matching helper.
- scripts/preflight.sh, scripts/services-setup.sh                →
  same call-site migration for install-time preflight checks.

Per-file behavior changes (apart from the mechanical substitution):

- `*_getent_passwd_entry` now accepts an empty argument to mean "dump
  the entire passwd table", and falls back to a pure-bash /etc/passwd
  scan when `getent` is absent or returns a nonzero rc that isn't
  user-not-found. Consumers that iterate the passwd table (e.g.
  `state_resolve_current_home`, `state_resolve_target_home`,
  the shell-switching branch in `scripts/lib/zsh.sh`) now work on
  Ubuntu/Debian minbase images where `getent` is not installed.
- New `*_passwd_home_from_entry` helper replaces inline
  `cut -d: -f6 <<< "$entry"` call-chains. The helper uses bash's own
  `IFS=: read -r` instead of forking `cut`, and runs the result
  through each script's existing `*_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path` guard
  so root (`/`), empty, and non-absolute passwd homes are rejected
  before anything writes to them.
- Consumers of `*_resolve_current_user` now honor its new rc: if the
  function cannot resolve a user at all (both `id` and `whoami`
  missing or broken), the calling site fails loudly or skips the
  downstream mutation rather than substituting an empty string into
  a path and chowning the wrong thing.
- Where appropriate, consumers that previously called
  `whoami 2>/dev/null` directly (e.g. shell-change detection in
  `scripts/lib/zsh.sh`, pwd-entry lookup in `scripts/acfs-global`)
  now resolve the user once via the helper and compare string
  against the resolved value, avoiding repeated forks.

No behavior change is intended on systems where `/usr/bin/id`,
`/usr/bin/whoami`, and `/usr/bin/getent` are all present and working.
The migration is a strict hardening for install surfaces that run
before a sane login environment exists (root-on-clean-Debian,
containers, sparse Ubuntu base images, broken /etc/nsswitch.conf
configurations).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

+_agent_system_binary_path() {
+    local name="${1:-}"
+    local candidate=""
+
+    [[ -n "$name" ]] || return 1
+
+    for candidate in \
+        "/usr/bin/$name" \
+        "/bin/$name" \
+        "/usr/sbin/$name" \
+        "/sbin/$name"
+    do
+        [[ -x "$candidate" ]] || continue
+        printf '%s\n' "$candidate"
+        return 0
+    done
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+_agent_resolve_current_user() {
+    local current_user=""
+    local id_bin=""
+    local whoami_bin=""
+
+    id_bin="$(_agent_system_binary_path id 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -n "$id_bin" ]]; then
+        current_user="$("$id_bin" -un 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -z "$current_user" ]]; then
+        whoami_bin="$(_agent_system_binary_path whoami 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        if [[ -n "$whoami_bin" ]]; then
+            current_user="$("$whoami_bin" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    [[ -n "$current_user" ]] || return 1
+    printf '%s\n' "$current_user"
+}
+
+_agent_getent_passwd_entry() {
+    local user="${1-}"
+    local getent_bin=""
+    local passwd_entry=""
+    local passwd_line=""
+    local printed_any=false
+
+    getent_bin="$(_agent_system_binary_path getent 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -z "$user" ]]; then
+        if [[ -n "$getent_bin" ]]; then
+            while IFS= read -r passwd_line; do
+                printf '%s\n' "$passwd_line"
+                printed_any=true
+            done < <("$getent_bin" passwd 2>/dev/null || true)
+            if [[ "$printed_any" == true ]]; then
+                return 0
+            fi
+        fi
+
+        [[ -r /etc/passwd ]] || return 1
+        while IFS= read -r passwd_line; do
+            printf '%s\n' "$passwd_line"
+        done < /etc/passwd
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -n "$getent_bin" ]]; then
+        passwd_entry="$("$getent_bin" passwd "$user" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -z "$passwd_entry" ]] && [[ -r /etc/passwd ]]; then
+        while IFS= read -r passwd_line; do
+            [[ "${passwd_line%%:*}" == "$user" ]] || continue
+            passwd_entry="$passwd_line"
+            break
+        done < /etc/passwd
+    fi
+
+    [[ -n "$passwd_entry" ]] || return 1
+    printf '%s\n' "$passwd_entry"
+}
+
+_agent_passwd_home_from_entry() {
+    local passwd_entry="${1:-}"
+    local passwd_home=""
+
+    [[ -n "$passwd_entry" ]] || return 1
+    IFS=: read -r _ _ _ _ _ passwd_home _ <<< "$passwd_entry"
+    passwd_home="$(_agent_existing_abs_home "$passwd_home" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    [[ -n "$passwd_home" ]] || return 1
+    printf '%s\n' "$passwd_home"
+}

+autofix_system_binary_path() {
+    local name="${1:-}"
+    local candidate=""
+
+    [[ -n "$name" ]] || return 1
+
+    for candidate in         "/usr/bin/$name"         "/bin/$name"         "/usr/local/bin/$name"         "/usr/local/sbin/$name"         "/usr/sbin/$name"         "/sbin/$name"; do
+        [[ -x "$candidate" ]] || continue
+        printf '%s\n' "$candidate"
+        return 0
+    done
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+autofix_resolve_current_user() {
+    local current_user=""
+    local id_bin=""

+
+    id_bin="$(autofix_system_binary_path id 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -n "$id_bin" ]]; then
+        current_user="$("$id_bin" -un 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -z "$current_user" ]]; then
+        whoami_bin="$(autofix_system_binary_path whoami 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        if [[ -n "$whoami_bin" ]]; then
+            current_user="$("$whoami_bin" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        fi
+    fi
